Bright Data Hackathon · May 2026

Taste Engine

Neighborhood-aware culinary intelligence

Turning local taste signals into operational menu strategy for fast-casual brands.

1 / 12
0:00 – 0:30 · Cold open

Brands run quarterly menu planning

Chipotle shortened its limited-time-offer cycle. CAVA does four seasonal menus a year. The bottleneck isn't creativity — it's neighborhood-level intelligence.

2 / 12
0:30 – 1:00 · Bright Data dependency

Powered by Bright Data

64,372 dated indie reviews · 383 restaurants · 3 metro markets · 24 months. Four Bright Data products: Google Maps Reviews Dataset, Fast Maps Search, SERP API, Scraping Browser.

3 / 12
1:00 – 2:00 · Act 1

Trend Maturity Spread

Indie restaurants tagged into competitive (lunch) vs leading (chef-driven) pools. The split between pools is structural and tells the maturity story.

4 / 12
1:00 – 2:00 · Act 1 continued

Same flavor, three cities, three stages

Williamsburg al pastor: 3 competitive, 8 leading → Rising, premature for chains. Mission: 58 competitive, 1 leading → Established, ship-now zone. WeHo: Established but thin.

5 / 12
2:00 – 3:00 · Act 2

Validation: the system caught the shift before the brand announced

Between Q4 2025 and Q1 2026, al pastor crossed Weak → Established in Mission indie reviews. On January 27, Chipotle announced the al pastor relaunch for February 10. The classifier flipped first.

6 / 12
2:00 – 3:00 · Act 2 continued

The next call: Mole gap-fill for Chipotle in Mission

Mole is the strongest mover in Mission this quarter — +7 mentions, 132 total. None of the chains can deliver it from current pantry. Confidence 68. Peak maturity. Low operational lift, national rollout. Californios verified as the comp.

7 / 12
3:00 – 3:45 · Act 3

Quarterly cadence — what changed and what's next

Every visit shows movers up (mole +7, al pastor +6, pesto +5), maturity transitions, and a directional forecast. Brands come back monthly for the deltas, not a one-shot analysis.

8 / 12
3:00 – 3:45 · Act 3 continued

Next-quarter forecasts with confidence bands

Mission birria forecast to rise to ~27 mentions in Q2 (±2). Multi-snapshot Trends velocity and cross-city diffusion predictions are explicit v2 roadmap — accruing now.

9 / 12
3:45 – 4:15 · Naive LLM vs Taste Engine

Eight hallucination classes caught. Naive LLM: zero.

Same model. Same prompt. The naive LLM hallucinated nine SKUs across two dishes and reinvented al pastor as a vegan mushroom dish — completely missing the actual brand decision happening live. The LLM is a reasoning layer; the pipeline is the innovation.

10 / 12
Recommendations surface

Six recommendations per neighborhood — every claim tool-validated

Two cards per brand × three brands. Each card carries confidence (0-100), maturity stage, lift tier, rollout portability, proven badge with newsroom URLs, and comp restaurant verification.

11 / 12
4:15 – 4:45 · Close

Taste Engine

Neighborhood-aware culinary intelligence

Built on Bright Data. Quarterly intelligence brief for brand strategy teams. Demo coverage: Chipotle, CAVA, Sweetgreen across Mission District, Williamsburg, West Hollywood.
